CITY OF FOUNTAIN VALLEY
May L | 1990
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COIOiISSION
P.O. Box 8O7
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attn: 
CATHRYN DONOVAN, ESQ
RE: OPINION REQUEST PURSUANT TO
GOVERNMENT CODE SECTTON 83114 (b)
Dear Ms. Donovan:
Request is hereby made for an opinion pursuant to Government Code
Section 83114(b). 
This request is being made on behalf of
Councilman James D. Petrikin.
Councilman Petrikin is a chiropractor who operates a chiropractic
clinic in the City of Fountain VaIIey. He has been in practice in
this City for over fourteen (14) years and has been active in the
Chamber of Commerce and many other loca1 organizations long before
he was a Council member. He provides chiropractic care to numerous
citizens of Fountain Va1ley. Some of these citizens are owners or
employees of local businesses that have health insurance pIans.
Those persons may come to Dr. Petrikin for treatment and Dr.
Petrikin may be paid by their health insurance company.
Dr. Petrikin now proposes to contact employers within the City to
make his services available through their insurance plan. In other
words, he wilI be actively marketing his services and promoting his
clinic to these employers. These companies would be those that
provide medicar/hearth care coverage to their employees by way of
an insurance poricy. some of the companies might only havg two (z)
or three (3) employees while other companies may have in excess of
Loo employees. He intends to contact these employers and request
that they authorize him to provide each emproyee wi€n a
chiropractic care identification 
card that Dr. pelrikin will
prepare. These cards will then be distributed to each employee
through company channels.
should an employee use such a card to obtain chiropractic care
through Dr. petrikin's crinic, the charge for care prtvided would
be billed to the insurance company in thL same manner as any other
patient with insurance. Dr. petrikin would receive no puy*"nt from
the employer's companies. payment would be directly irom the

1O2OO SLATER AVENUE FOUNTAIN VALLEY
i: 7t r i,r


rAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COIi{II{ISSION
cathryn Donovan, Esq.
May 1, 1990

insurance conpany to Dr. Petrj.kin's clinic. 
It is assumed that the
enrployer and/ or erRployee would pay the insurance premiums and this
rnoney would go directly to the insurance conpany.
The specific question that Councj.lnan Petrikin asks is whether any
amount of insurance payments paid to Dr. Petrikin by the employee's
insurance conpany can be considered a rrsource of incomerr to Dr.
Petrikin that is attributed to the employer company? Specifically,
Dr. Petrikin has nentioned the possibility 
of having such an
arrangement with Rainbow Disposal conpany which ernploys about 170
enployees. Rainbow Disposal has the city's 
trash collection
franchise. If Dr. Petrikin obtains paynents from Rainbow Disposal
Cornpany's insurance conpany for providing services to its
enployees, could those payments be considered a trsource of inconert
to Dr. Petrikin that would be attributed to Rainbow Disposal
company, that could require that he disqualify himself when voting
on Rainbo!{'s exclusive trash collection franchise?
If so, $rhat are the facts that make this situation distinguishable
from the one in \.rhich Dr. Petrikin treats a patient who is an
insured employee, receives payment fron the insurance conpany, and
then votes on the enployer's item on the agenda?
Your advice and assistance in this rnatter is appreciated. I
understand that this may be a question of first impression for your
agency so Dr. Petrikin intends to awai.t your response before
proceeding.
If I can provide further infornation on this natter, pfease feel-
free to caII.
very truly yours,
0/^*,(.a,-,;
Alan R. Burns.a/
city Attorney
ARB : 15

sections.question: The specific question that Councilman Petrikin asks is whether any amount of insurance payments paid to Dr. Petrikin by the employee's insurance company can be considered a "source of income" to Dr. Petrikin that is attributed to the employer company? Specifically, Dr. Petrikin has mentioned the possibility of having such an arrangement with Rainbow Disposal company which employs about 170 employees. Rainbow Disposal has the city's trash collection franchise. If Dr. Petrikin obtains payments from Rainbow Disposal Company's insurance company for providing services to its employees, could those payments be considered a "source of income" to Dr. Petrikin that would be attributed to Rainbow Disposal company, that could require that he disqualify himself when voting on Rainbow's exclusive trash collection franchise? If so, what are the facts that make this situation distinguishable from the one in which Dr. Petrikin treats a patient who is an insured employee, receives payment from the insurance company, and then votes on the employer's item on the agenda?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether insurance payments received by Councilman Petrikin for chiropractic services provided to employees of companies with city contracts (specifically Rainbow Disposal) should be attributed to those employers as a source of income requiring disqualification from voting on their matters.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. The question section is clearly delineated in the document. Minor OCR errors noted (e.g., 'crinic' for 'clinic', 'Rainbow' partially obscured). Document represents a request for opinion pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The specific question that Councilman Petrikin asks is whether any amount of insurance payments paid to Dr. Petrikin by the employee's insurance company can be considered a "source of income" to Dr. Petrikin that is attributed to the employer company? Specifically, Dr. Petrikin has mentioned the possibility of having such an arrangement with Rainbow Disposal company which employs about 170 employees. Rainbow Disposal has the city's trash collection franchise. If Dr. Petrikin obtains payments from Rainbow Disposal Company's insurance company for providing services to its employees, could those payments be considered a "source of income" to Dr. Petrikin that would be attributed to Rainbow Disposal company, that could require that he disqualify himself when voting on Rainbow's exclusive trash collection franchise? If so, what are the facts that make this situation distinguishable from the one in which Dr. Petrikin treats a patient who is an insured employee, receives payment from the insurance company, and then votes on the employer's item on the agenda?

embedding.summary: City Attorney Alan Burns requests an FPPC opinion on behalf of Councilman James Petrikin, a chiropractor, regarding whether income from insurance payments for treating employees of companies with city contracts constitutes a reportable source of income requiring disqualification from voting on those companies' matters.
